tipRules.tip: Hackney: 'If you see a mat made out of anything, flip it. Don't think about the season or weather. Mats are some of the most reliable places to catch a bass.'
Hackney: 'Change weight sizes based on hardness of the mat top. Leaves with no wind — go light. Dead grass baked hard by the sun — upsize your weight.'

knowledge.insight: Hackney: 'Do not set the hook with a jerk when flipping mats — the weight and plastic will ball up and you'll never get a good hookset. Reel down until you feel the fish, then use a steady, hard, strong, quick pull back to drive the hook home.'
Hackney: 'Pull the fish back with the rod — don't try to crank her back. The reel's only function is to keep slack out of the line.'
Hackney: 'Flipping mats absolutely requires the right rod, reel and line. Most techniques you can do with several different combinations and still catch fish. Not when you're flipping mats.'
Hackney: 'Visualize the hookset in your mind before every flip. If you don't, you'll reflexively jerk your rod when you get a bite.'
